definition: S&P 500 index concentration (top 10 = ~35%) potentially distorts measured stock-bond correlation. Mega-cap tech with exceptional ROIC and net-cash balance sheets may mask the broader S&P 493 already being in robustly positive correlation territory.
evidence: equity_analyst_02 bifurcated analogue: mega-cap ≈ 1994-2000, broad market ≈ 1967-1982
Multiple agents raise the issue but none resolve it empirically
No agent provides equal-weight SPX correlation test
Flagged as highest-priority data question for future research
open_questions: Whether equal-weight SPX correlation with bonds looks meaningfully different
How concentration changes over time affect aggregate correlation measurement
